name.description: Name of the ServiceLbPolicy resource. It matches pattern `projects/{project}/locations/{location}/serviceLbPolicies/{service_lb_policy_name}`.

location.description: The location of the service lb policy.

labels.description: Set of label tags associated with the ServiceLbPolicy resource. **Note**: This field is non-authoritative, and will only manage the labels present in your configuration. Please refer to the field `effective_labels` for all of the labels present on the resource.

description.description: A free-text description of the resource. Max length 1024 characters.

load_balancing_algorithm.description: The type of load balancing algorithm to be used. The default behavior is WATERFALL_BY_REGION. Possible values are: `SPRAY_TO_REGION`, `SPRAY_TO_WORLD`, `WATERFALL_BY_REGION`, `WATERFALL_BY_ZONE`.

auto_capacity_drain.description: Option to specify if an unhealthy MIG/NEG should be considered for global load balancing and traffic routing. Structure is [documented below](#nested_auto_capacity_drain).

enable.description: Optional. If set to 'True', an unhealthy MIG/NEG will be set as drained. - An MIG/NEG is considered unhealthy if less than 25% of the instances/endpoints in the MIG/NEG are healthy. - This option will never result in draining more than 50% of the configured IGs/NEGs for the Backend Service.

failover_config.description: Option to specify health based failover behavior. This is not related to Network load balancer FailoverPolicy. Structure is [documented below](#nested_failover_config).

failover_health_threshold.description: Optional. The percentage threshold that a load balancer will begin to send traffic to failover backends. If the percentage of endpoints in a MIG/NEG is smaller than this value, traffic would be sent to failover backends if possible. This field should be set to a value between 1 and 99. The default value is 50 for Global external HTTP(S) load balancer (classic) and Proxyless service mesh, and 70 for others.

project.description: If it is not provided, the provider project is used.
